---
title: "Building a connector"
description: "Implement the Connector interface to ingest a new source into typed memory."
---

A **connector** ingests data from an external system (Notion, Linear, Gmail, Drive, GitHub) and emits `MemoryProposal` rows into the typed memory schema. BBC ships five connectors out of the box; this page is how you write a sixth.

If you only need to import a single skill or template, see [Importing a skill](/build/import-skill) — that's a much lighter path. Connectors are for **streams of facts**, not one-shot imports.

## What a connector does

A connector is a TypeScript module that implements the `Connector` interface in `apps/dashboard/src/lib/connectors/framework.ts`. The framework's `runSync()` orchestrator handles the operational glue every connector shares:

- Token refresh < 24h before OAuth expiry.
- 429 backoff with exponential delay + jitter.
- Cursor persistence in `tenant_connectors.sync_state`.
- Partial-failure commit (proposals collected before an error stay; status becomes `partial`).
- `source_ref` dedup against `memory_files.fields.source_ref`.
- `max_proposals_per_sync` cap.

You write the OAuth handshake, the API walk, and the mapping from provider records to typed memories. The framework writes the rest.

## The interface

```ts
export interface Connector {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  writes_to: Supertag[];
  oauth_scopes?: string[];
  permission_summary: string;

  authenticate(tenant_id: string, redirect_url: string): Promise<AuthURL>;
  complete_auth(tenant_id: string, code: string): Promise<{ external_account_id: string }>;
  refresh_token?(external_account_id: string): Promise<void>;

  sync(ctx: SyncContext): AsyncIterable<SyncEvent>;
  preview?(ctx: SyncContext): Promise<MemoryProposal[]>;

  sync_schedule: "on_demand" | { interval_minutes: number };
  max_proposals_per_sync: number;
  rate_limit_strategy: RateLimitStrategy;
}
```

## Required steps

### 1. OAuth handshake

Implement `authenticate(tenant_id, redirect_url)` and `complete_auth(tenant_id, code)`. Use the shared `google-oauth.ts` helper if you're integrating a Google product; otherwise mirror the provider's authorize-then-exchange flow.

Store the token in `external_accounts` and return its `external_account_id`. The framework will call `refresh_token(external_account_id)` automatically when expiry is < 24h away — implement it if your provider issues short-lived tokens.

```ts
async authenticate(tenant_id, redirect_url) {
  const state = encodeState(tenant_id);
  return {
    url: `https://provider.com/oauth/authorize?...&state=${state}`,
    state,
  };
}
```

### 2. The sync generator

`sync(ctx)` is an `AsyncIterable<SyncEvent>`. Yield three event kinds:

- `{ kind: "proposal", proposal }` — a `MemoryProposal` to commit.
- `{ kind: "checkpoint", cursor }` — persist the cursor so a mid-sync failure resumes from here.
- `{ kind: "rate_limit", retry_after_ms }` — pause the iterator. The framework sleeps with backoff + jitter and resumes.

```ts
async *sync(ctx) {
  let cursor = ctx.cursor;
  while (true) {
    const page = await fetchPage(cursor, ctx);
    if (page.rateLimited) {
      yield { kind: "rate_limit", retry_after_ms: page.retryAfterMs };
      continue;
    }
    for (const item of page.items) {
      yield { kind: "proposal", proposal: mapToProposal(item) };
    }
    cursor = page.nextCursor;
    yield { kind: "checkpoint", cursor };
    if (!cursor) break;
  }
}
```

<Note>
  Rate-limit signaling is **yield-based**, not throw-based. Throwing exhausts an
  async generator and can't resume. Yield `rate_limit` events instead.
</Note>

### 3. Map to typed memories

Each `MemoryProposal` carries:

```ts
type MemoryProposal = {
  type: Supertag;              // 'decision' | 'voice' | 'glossary' | ...
  title: string;
  body: string;
  fields: Record<string, unknown>;
  source_ref: string;          // provider's stable ID; framework dedupes on this
};
```

Map provider fields to the typed schema. Default to `note` if you can't infer a stronger supertag — never invent supertags. The 9-supertag enum is fixed; see [memory types](/concepts/memory-types).

Use a stable, opaque provider ID for `source_ref`. Examples:

- Notion: `notion:page:<page_id>`
- Linear: `linear:issue:<issue_id>`
- Gmail: `gmail:thread:<thread_id>` or `gmail:contact:<email>` for team rows
- GitHub: `github:<owner>/<repo>:<sha>:<path>`

The framework dedupes `(tenant_id, source_ref)` before commit. If the row already exists, the proposal is skipped silently.

### 4. Register in the catalog

Add the connector descriptor to `memory/ops/connectors/` (file-mode) or `connector_catalog` (DB-mode):

```yaml
id: your-connector
name: "Your Provider"
description: "What the connector does, one sentence."
permission_summary: "What we read; what we don't touch."
writes_to: [decision, note]
oauth_scopes:
  - read:resource
sync_schedule:
  interval_minutes: 60
```

The `permission_summary` is the line shown on the install card. Lead with **what we read**; be explicit about **what we don't touch** (e.g., "we do not read DMs", "we do not modify files").

### 5. Wire the route

Add an entry to `src/app/api/connectors/[id]/route.ts` (OAuth callback) and `src/lib/connectors/index.ts` (registry export). The framework's `installConnector()` handles the admin gate.

## Testing

Mirror the existing connector tests:

- `apps/dashboard/src/lib/connectors/<your>.test.ts` — unit tests with an injected fetch.
- Use the `ConnectorDb` port from `framework.ts` to test against an in-memory implementation; no Supabase needed.
- Cover: empty result page, paginated walk, 429 backoff, auth-expired error, partial failure mid-sync.

Five reference connectors live in `apps/dashboard/src/lib/connectors/`:

- `notion.ts` — non-expiring tokens, opaque cursor.
- `linear.ts` — GraphQL, issue→decision mapping.
- `gmail.ts` — `threads.list` with label-driven supertag.
- `drive.ts` — `files.list` across all drives, per-mime export.
- `github.ts` — repo+path walk for SKILL.md ingest.

## Webhooks (optional)

If your provider supports webhooks, add a handler at `src/app/api/webhooks/<your>/route.ts`. On failure, write to `webhook_dead_letters` with a typed reason — the `/library/diagnostics` admin page reads from that table.

## Checklist

Before shipping a connector:

- [ ] OAuth round-trip works end-to-end against a live tenant.
- [ ] Cursor persists across syncs (kill mid-walk, restart, no duplicates).
- [ ] 429 backoff yields and resumes; no thrown rate-limit errors inside the generator.
- [ ] `source_ref` dedup works (re-run a sync, see `skipped_duplicates` go up, `emitted` stays 0).
- [ ] `permission_summary` is honest. If you write outside `writes_to`, the type checker breaks.
- [ ] Test floor parity with reference connectors.
- [ ] `tenant_connectors.last_sync_status` shows `auth_expired` after a token revoke (not `error`).
